reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based on CMakeLists.txt downloading dependencies, but this is redundant and unused during AUR builds since PKGBUILD already fetches and verifies raylib. No actual malicious behavior exists.
disagreement_analysis: deepseek skipped due to download failure; qwen overreacted to CMakeLists.txt dependency fetch that doesn't execute in AUR context.
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review any files due to makepkg failure
qwen skipped binary file raylib-6.0.tar.gz (should verify PKGBUILD checksum instead)
false_positives: CMakeLists.txt flagged unsafe: Download command is dead code during AUR builds since PKGBUILD already provides verified tarball
git describe in CMakeLists.txt: Normal versioning pattern, not security-relevant
learnings: Improve network resilience: Retry failed source downloads before skipping
Contextualize build scripts: Flag network access only if it executes during makepkg
Prioritize PKGBUILD checksum verification over binary file skipping
Suppress false alarms on dead code paths in build systems
